pull_request.title: Add synthetic Python tests covering many Python constructs
pull_request.body:
This PR adds 326 Python tests that cover many Python constructs. Each test is simple and follows the same structure:
```python
def some_function():
   # do some computation
   assert(...)       # This assert must be proven valid

some_function()
```
The tests that can be analyzed with Strata and for which the assert is proven valid (99 tests) are in the `tests/` directory, with an `expected` file. The tests that do not yet pass with Strata (either failing with internal errors or the assert cannot be proven) are in the `pending/` directory, and are not launched by the CI test framework.

By submitting this pull request, I confirm that you can use, modify, copy, and redistribute this contribution, under the terms of your choice.

+      - name: Run pyAnalyze SARIF tests
         working-directory: StrataTest/Languages/Python
         shell: bash
-        run: |
-          ./run_py_analyze.sh
-          python run_py_analyze_sarif.py --laurel
+        run: python run_py_analyze_sarif.py --laurel
+      - name: Run pyAnalyze golden-file tests
+        working-directory: StrataTest/Languages/Python
+        shell: bash
+        run: ./run_py_analyze.sh
       - name: Run regex differential tests
         working-directory: StrataTest/Languages/Python/Regex
         run: python diff_test.py
 
+  check_pending_python:
+    needs: build_and_test_lean
+    name: Check pending Python tests
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    permissions:
+      contents: read
+    steps:
+    - uses: actions/checkout@v6
+    - uses: actions/setup-python@v6
+      with:
+        python-version: '3.14'
+    - name: Build using pip
+      run: pip install .
+      working-directory: Tools/Python
+    - name: Restore Lean build cache
+      uses: actions/cache/restore@v5
+      with:
+        path: .lake
+        key: lake-${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-${{ hashFiles('lean-toolchain') }}-${{ hashFiles('lake-manifest.json') }}-${{ hashFiles('**/*.st') }}-${{ github.sha }}
+        fail-on-cache-miss: true
+    - name: Install Lean
+      uses: leanprover/lean-action@v1
+      with:
+        auto-config: false
+        build: false
+    - name: Install cvc5
+      shell: bash
+      run: |
+            ARCH=$(uname -m)
+            if [ "$ARCH" = "x86_64" ]; then ARCH_NAME="x86_64"
+            elif [ "$ARCH" = "aarch64" ] || [ "$ARCH" = "arm64" ]; then ARCH_NAME="arm64"
+            else echo "Unsupported architecture: $ARCH"; exit 1; fi
+            wget -q https://github.com/cvc5/cvc5/releases/download/cvc5-1.2.1/cvc5-Linux-${ARCH_NAME}-static.zip
+            unzip -q cvc5-Linux-${ARCH_NAME}-static.zip
+            sudo cp cvc5-Linux-${ARCH_NAME}-static/bin/cvc5 /usr/local/bin/
+    - name: Check pending tests for newly passing
+      working-directory: StrataTest/Languages/Python
+      shell: bash
+      run: ./run_py_analyze.sh --check-pending
+
   lint_checks:
     name: Run lint checks
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest

+        parse_exit=$?

+    done

+    if [ $pending_total -gt 0 ]; then

+    i: int = 2

+    return b
+

+    assert fib(1) == 1, "fib 1"

+    i: int = 2

+        i = i + 1
+    return True

+class FakeCtx:
+    entered: bool
+    exited: bool
+
+    def __init__(self):
+        self.entered = False
+        self.exited = False
+
+    def __enter__(self):
+        self.entered = True
+        return self
+
+    def __exit__(self, *args):
+        self.exited = True
+
+def test():
+    ctx = FakeCtx()
+    with ctx as c:
+        assert c.entered == True, "entered"
+    assert ctx.exited == True, "exited"
+test()

commit_summaries.message:
chore(python-tests): Import 328 benchmark tests and add --pending flag to runner

Import 326 Python test files from benchmarks (2 skipped due to
existing files) and generate 106 new expected output files for
tests that fully pass. Update run_py_analyze.sh with --pending
flag that runs tests without expected files and reports their
status without affecting the exit code.

Test counts: 382 total test files, 156 with expected output,
227 pending (77 error, 150 imprecise).

fix(test-runner): Address review feedback for pending test support

- Add Pending (pass) category for pending tests that succeed cleanly,
  with a separate pending_pass counter (was misclassified as "unknown"
  and lumped into pending_imprecise)
- Update summary line to report all three categories:
  error, imprecise, pass
- Check py_to_strata exit code in pending path and classify parser
  failures as Pending (parse error) vs Pending (analysis error)
- Stop suppressing stderr from py_to_strata in pending path so parser
  diagnostics are visible
- Add TODO for --promote flag to auto-generate .expected files for
  passing pending tests

chore(python-tests): Move pending tests to tests/pending/ subdirectory

Move 227 test files without expected output into a dedicated
tests/pending/ directory. Update run_py_analyze.sh to scan
tests/pending/ when --pending flag is used, keeping the main
test loop focused on tests with expected files only.

fix(test): Address review feedback for Move pending tests to tests/pending/ subdirectory

- Rename missing_init.py → tests/pending/test_missing_init.py so it
  follows the test_* naming convention and is exercised by the pending
  test loop
- Add trailing newline to test_missing_init.py (POSIX compliance)
- Move missing_required_param.py → tests/pending/test_missing_required_param.py
  (same naming issue, not called out in review but identical problem)
- Fix memory doc: clarify "155 test_*.py files" instead of misleading
  "155 .py files", update pending count from 227 → 229
chore: Remove changelog docs from PR branch

Fail CI when pending Python tests start passing; share Lean build cache

Add --check-pending flag to run_py_analyze.sh that runs pending tests
and fails if any now pass. This prevents the pending list from going
stale — when a bug fix or feature makes a pending test pass, CI forces
the developer to promote it to an active test with an expected file.

CI structure:
- build_and_test_lean: builds Lean, saves cache, runs golden-file +
  SARIF tests (sequential, fast)
- check_pending_python: parallel job that restores the Lean cache and
  runs --check-pending (can take minutes with 242 pending tests)
- build_python (3.11-3.14 matrix): restores Lean cache, runs only
  Python-version-sensitive tests

Also moves 15 tests without expected files from tests/ to pending/.
